title: strychnos angolensis var. likimiensis
intro: Strychnos angolensis var. likimiensis, also known as Likima poison or Likima fruit, is a plant species found in tropical Africa, particularly in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The plant's roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including antimalarial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Strychnos angolensis var. likimiensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable herbal remedy. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and the roots are also used to make a powder that can be used as a topical treatment.
